original_text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering a path to citizenship for 10 million undocumented immigrants.
identified_property: comprehensiveness and quality of the citizenship pathway

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering 10 million undocumented immigrants only short-term renewable work permits with no pathway to permanent residency or citizenship and no protection from future deportation.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering 10 million undocumented immigrants a narrow and restrictive path to legal residency only, with stringent eligibility criteria, high fees, and no guarantee of eventual citizenship.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering 10 million undocumented immigrants a lengthy and conditional path to citizenship with extensive waiting periods of 15+ years, substantial fees, and strict eligibility requirements that many may struggle to meet.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering a path to citizenship for 10 million undocumented immigrants.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering 10 million undocumented immigrants a clear and accessible path to citizenship with reasonable timelines, affordable application fees, and protections against deportation and family separation throughout the process.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering 10 million undocumented immigrants a comprehensive path to citizenship with expedited processing, government-funded legal assistance, fee waivers for low-income applicants, full access to social services during the transition, and robust anti-deportation protections.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A U.S. federal immigration reform bill is passed, offering 10 million undocumented immigrants a fully streamlined and inclusive path to citizenship with rapid processing, free legal representation, complete access to public benefits during transition, funded language and employment integration programs, retroactive protections, and automatic eligibility extensions for family members.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier maintains the same core scenario (a federal bill covering 10 million undocumented immigrants) while progressively improving the quality, accessibility, and comprehensiveness of the citizenship pathway—from bare temporary permits (tier 1) through a standard path (tier 4) to a fully supported, streamlined, and inclusive process (tier 7).

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a more comprehensive and higher-quality citizenship pathway—with faster processing, legal support, financial accessibility, integration services, and family protections—maximizes the real-world benefit of the reform for the affected population.
